title: Cephalocarpus clarkei
intro: Cephalocarpus clarkei, also known as Sikkim's tree fern or Indian tree fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cephalocotaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, respiratory problems, and digestive disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cephalocarpus clarkei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and powders, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cephalocarpus clarkei is classified as a member of the family Stemonuraceae, which is a family of flowering plants in the order Theales. It belongs to the genus Cephalocarpus, which is a small group of plants. The family Stemonuraceae is part of the larger clade of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Cephalocarpus clarkei is native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia, where it grows as a tree. Its classification is still being studied and refined by botanists and taxonomists.
